title: Global DANS display controller and command-palette boundary
summary: dans_mode owns global frontend/requested-monochrome/effective-monochrome state, while dans_menu is a declarative owner-facing inventory that dispatches tools only after restoring its captured source context.
details: dans_mode defines effective monochrome as frontend_enabled OR monochrome_requested and exposes the lock directly instead of scattering equivalent conditions across menu and colorscheme code.

Applying frontend state terminates tokenizer mode first, then drives every umbrella renderer, doc markdown, discovered-macro coloring, and scope coloring through explicit idempotent per-buffer setters.

Normal-color mode restarts the Tree-sitter highlighter; effective monochrome stops only highlighting while leaving parsers available to presentation and tooling. The Tree-sitter plugin's flatten/disable callbacks consult the same controller.
FileType and BufWinEnter hooks apply the current global state to future C/C++/CUDA buffers, while an explicit transition updates all loaded language buffers.
dans_menu builds one list of sections and actions, captures the originating window and buffer, and closes before invoking any workflow that expects a source context.

COMMAND_COVERAGE is a test-visible inventory boundary for registered Dans commands. Aggregated low-level renderer commands map to the frontend item; dedicated generators map to the generation chooser.
dans_vanilla delegates to the new controller solely for compatibility and is no longer an independent state owner.
rationale: One state owner prevents menu labels, extmark modules, Tree-sitter highlighting, and colorscheme flattening from reporting different modes.
Idempotent aggregate application makes startup, newly opened buffers, toggles, and compatibility calls share one lifecycle path.
Separating declarative menu inventory from target-context dispatch keeps future tools discoverable without teaching each one about floating-window state.

provenance.note: Current display ownership, compatibility, menu, and highlighting integration.
The first interaction run exposed asymmetric nil-default setters in doc, macro, and scope modules; explicit branches and re-enable assertions now pin symmetry.

evidence.claim: Controller state, module aggregation, highlighting, remembered preference, locked interaction, target dispatch, font input, and command inventory are exercised through the real UI.

questions: Which module owns whether the frontend and monochrome are active?
How is the current display choice applied to a newly opened C++ buffer?
Why does the menu close before launching assembly or project inspection?
Where must a new Dans command be added so the palette exposes it?

last_verified.method: Source audit plus focused headless display-mode and command-palette interaction coverage.
